News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Trump Says US Raid on Venezuela Yielded Five-Fold Profit Sputnik 27 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said that the operation in Venezuela proved financially beneficial for the United States.
  • Venezuelan Diplomats Arrive in Washington for Talks with US State Department Sputnik 27 Mar 2026 -- Venezuelan diplomats are presently in Washington DC on a working visit, Vice Minister Oliver Blanco announced, after wrapping up initial talks with US State Department counterparts.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Three Islands That Could Be Key In Keeping The Strait Of Hormuz Open RFE/RL 27 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has warned of possible further action against Kharg Island, a key oil terminal of Iran and a major cog in the country's economic machine.

  • War against Iran draining US Tomahawk missile stockpile, alarming Pentagon: Report Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- US military has fired more than 850 Tomahawk cruise missiles in four weeks of its war against Iran, burning through the precision weapons at a rate that has alarmed Pentagon officials and prompted internal discussions about how to make more available, according to a report by The Washington Post, citing sources.
  • US-Israel missile defence on brink as interceptor stocks near 'critical depletion': Report Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- A new report has warned that the United States and the Israeli regime are facing a logistical crisis in their military aggression against Iran, with interceptor inventories projected to run out within days or weeks.

  • Iran: Ardakan Yellow Cake Factory In Yazd Province Attacked RFE/RL 27 Mar 2026 -- The Atomic Energy Organization of Iran announced that the Ardakan yellow cake production plant in Yazd Province had been attacked on March 27./li>
  • Iran Reports Attacks On Arak Nuclear Facility, Steel Factories In Ahvaz, Isfahan RFE/RL 27 Mar 2026 -- The Arak Heavy Water Complex, known as Khondab, was hit "in two stages" on March 27, according to a local government official./li>
  • Bushehr nuclear plant hit for 3rd time amid ongoing US-Israeli aggression, no damage reported Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Atomic Energy Organization of Iran confirms that the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant in the country's south has been struck by a projectile, marking the third attack on the facility during ongoing unprovoked American-Israeli aggression targeting the Islamic Republic.
  • Two Iranian steel producers targeted in US-Israel attacks, casualties reported Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The United States and the Israeli regime have carried out separate attacks on two main Iranian steel companies in the central and southwestern provinces of Isfahan and Khuzestan.

  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 27 Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- On Friday, March 26, 2027, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Army conducted multiple operations as part of Operation True Promise 4, which was launched immediately after the US-Israeli coalition carried out an unprovoked act of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran on February 28.
  • 'Beyond eye for eye': IRGC urges evacuation of US, Israeli-linked sites after steel plant strike Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- A senior commander of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a stark warning that Tehran's retaliation will be beyond a simple proportional response, urging workers at industrial sites linked to the US and the Israeli regime to evacuate immediately to save their lives.
  • True Promise 4, Wave 84: IRGC missiles target US refueling planes at Al-Kharj Base Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) announced that it has successfully destroyed a number of US refueling vehicles and their logistical support fleet during the 84th wave of Operation True Promise 4.
  • Iranian Army drones strike major Israeli military logistics site Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army says it has targeted sensitive and strategic locations in Israel's Tel Aviv with suicide drones, almost four weeks after a US-Israeli military coalition began an unprovoked war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • IRGC urges regional residents to evacuate areas hosting US-Israeli troops Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a statement addressing people across West Asia, saying it is obligated to eliminate US and Israeli "terrorist forces" wherever they are found for indiscriminately killing Iranian civilians and targeting key figures.
  • Iranian air defenses intercept Tomahawk missile, down US-Israeli reconnaissance drone Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Iranian air defense forces intercepted an incoming Tomahawk cruise missile and shot down a US-Israeli reconnaissance drone in Western Iran, say officials, as the number of downed hostile drones has surpassed 130.
  • Iran Recruits 12-Year-Olds As 'Combatants Defending The Homeland' RFE/RL 27 Mar 2026 -- Iran has announced that children as young as 12 can sign up under a new program "for combatants defending the homeland," with applicants directed to mosques and "registration booths" on central squares.
  • Iran Mobilizing Over 1 Million Troops Amid Escalation — Reports Sputnik 27 Mar 2026 -- Iran has mobilized more than one million personnel for potential ground combat, while also seeing a surge in volunteers seeking to join the Basij militia, the IRGC, and the army, Tasnim reported, citing an informed source.
  • True Promise 4, Wave 83: Iran pounds US, Israeli assets with advanced missiles, drones Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) announced the execution of wave 83 of Operation True Promise 4 in the early hours of Friday, targeting key American and Israeli military installations across the region with missiles and drones.

  • Rubio Says US Expects To End Iran Campaign In 'Weeks, Not Months,' Can Achieve Goals Without Ground Troops RFE/RL 27 Mar 2026 -- The United States expects to wrap up its military operation in Iran within "weeks, not months" and believes it is possible to achieve its goals without ground troops, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on March 27.
  • House Armed Services Committe Democrats Call for Public Hearing on Iran War House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 27 Mar 2026 -- House Armed Services Committee (HASC) Ranking Member Adam Smith (D-Wash.) led a letter Friday signed by all HASC Democrats and sent to Chairman Mike Rogers (R-Ala.) asking that a public hearing be immediately convened and the Department of Defense be brought in to testify on President Trump's war against Iran.
  • Vance chided Netanyahu in call over 'overly optimistic' Iran war predictions: Report Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- A tense phone call this week between US vice president JD Vance and Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu has laid bare growing friction between Washington and Tel Aviv over an unplanned war that has proved disastrous for both allies.
  • Rubio Lands in France With Message for G7: Do More On Iran RFE/RL 27 Mar 2026 -- PARIS -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in France early on March 27, joining G7 foreign ministers and allied representatives who have already gathered for the second day of a high-stakes meeting overshadowed by tensions over Iran, Ukraine, and global security.
  • Trump claims Iran has allowed 10 oil vessels through Strait of Hormuz as "present" People's Daily 27 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump claimed on Thursday that Iran has allowed 10 "boats of oil" through the Strait of Hormuz, including eight bearing Pakistani flags, touting this as a "present" to the United States amid reported U.S.-Iran "indirect talks."
  • Trump's envoy confirms White House sent 15-point plan to Iran via Pakistan People's Daily 27 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump's envoy Steve Witkoff on Thursday confirmed that the White House has sent a 15-point plan to Tehran via Pakistan in an attempt to end the war with Iran.
  • Trump extends pause on striking Iranian energy plants by 10 days People's Daily 27 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday he will pause planned strikes on Iranian energy facilities for 10 days, claiming that talks between the two sides are "going very well."

  • Control of Hormuz Strait "legal right" of Iran: FM People's Daily 27 Mar 2026 -- As a coastal state, it is Iran's legal right to prevent vessels belonging to or affiliated with the United States, Israel and their allies from passing through the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi said on Thursday.
  • Iran serious about resolute defense of its sovereignty against ongoing aggression, FM tells Lavrov Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi asserts that Iran remains serious about the resolute defense of its national sovereignty amid continued unprovoked American-Israeli aggression.
  • Iran vows to exact 'heavy price' for Israeli attacks on civilian infrastructure Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi pledges that Iran will be exacting a "heavy price" for the Israeli regime's attacks on the Islamic Republic's civilian infrastructure.
  • Iran: Israeli assassination of Iranian diplomats in Lebanon testament to 'organized terrorism' Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian Foreign Ministry has strongly condemned the "heinous" Israeli assassination of Iranian diplomats in Lebanon, describing it as "a clear example of organized terrorism."
  • US cannot protect its troops on Iranian soil: Qalibaf Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf has questioned the United States' ability to protect its troops on Iranian soil, given its incapability of safeguarding its forces at its regional bases.
  • 'Appeasing bullies only wets their appetite': Tehran denounces Canada for new anti-Iran sanctions Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman has slammed Canada after it imposed new sanctions against the Islamic Republic amid the US-Israeli aggression, warning that their "bullying" will ultimately affect Ottawa.
  • IRGC busts Mossad-linked terrorist team in western Iran Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The intelligence organization of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has arrested a three-member team affiliated with the Israeli spy agency Mossad in the western Iranian province of Kermanshah.
  • Senior Iranian official warns IAEA's Grossi over 'biased, destructive' remarks Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- A senior Iranian diplomat has slammed International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi, warning that his recent "biased and destructive" remarks on Iran's peaceful nuclear program make the situation worse.
  • Iran urges UN to condemn aggressors, hold them accountable for war crimes Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian foreign minister has called on the United Nations to condemn the US and the Israeli regime for waging an illegal war on the Islamic Republic and hold the regimes accountable for their crimes.
  • Iranian, Russian Foreign Ministers Discuss Middle East Crisis Sputnik 27 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held a telephone conversation with Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to discuss the situation in the Middle East, the Iranian Foreign Ministry said on Friday.
  • Netanyahu forcing ordinary Americans to pay for war, Araghchi says as stocks slump Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says American taxpayer dollars and lives are the costs of Israeli prime minister Banjamin Netanyahu's "lost" gamble, amid almost four weeks of a US-Israeli unprovoked war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.

  • Russia's Medvedev: Ground invasion of Iran would be America's new Vietnam Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The deputy Chairman of Russia's Security Council has warned the US that the fate of any ground invasion of Iran would be a crushing defeat, adding that such a move would entrap Washington in a quagmire similar to the Vietnam War.

  • Note to Correspondents: on the Strait of Hormuz UN Secretary-General 27 Mar 2026

  • Joint Statement of G7 Foreign Ministers on Iran Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 27 Mar 2026
  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Urgent Debate called by Iran, China and Cuba European External Action Service (EEAS) 27 Mar 2026
  • "The price of this unlawful war is being paid by the entire humanity" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 27 Mar 2026 -- Speaking at the World Economic Forum Türkiye Strategy Meeting, President Erdoğan said: "The adverse effects of the war are being felt by the entire world in a wide range of areas from energy markets to the operation of production, informatics, transportation and trade networks. As I have previously stated on different occasions, the price of this meaningless, unlawful and unnecessary war, which has deeply shaken our region for one month, is being paid by the entire humanity as well as the parties to the conflicts."
  • Yemen declares readiness for direct military intervention unless US, Israel halt aggression Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Yemeni Armed Forces have warned that they are prepared for direct military intervention if American-Israeli aggression against Iran and the Axis of Resistance continues to escalate.
  • Joint Statement of G7 Foreign Ministers on Iran UK FCDO 27 Mar 2026 -- A joint statement of G7 Foreign Ministers following the G7 Foreign Ministerial Meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay
  • Ukraine, Canada, Sweden, and the United Kingdom jointly filed a counter-memorial against the Iran Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 27 Mar 2026

  • Iran War Will Reshape Dynamics Ahead Of Trump-Xi Summit, Says Former US Official RFE/RL 27 Mar 2026 -- A long-awaited meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping will take place in Beijing on May 14-15 after Trump said Washington asked to delay the summit originally planned for April due to the war with Iran.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Pentagon's New Chief Information Officer Details Strategy to Develop Tech, Cyber Warfighting Advantage DoD News 27 Mar 2026 -- During testimony at a pair of hearings on Capitol Hill this week, the War Department's new chief information officer outlined her office's strategy to transform technology and cybersecurity into a decisive warfighting advantage for the armed services.
  • US Army tests medical readiness tracker for the first time in Africa during Justified Accord 2026 Army News 27 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Army military medical professionals tested the Medical Currency Application for Readiness Tracking 2.0, a digital, field-medicine tracker, for the first time in Africa during Justified Accord 2026 at Lugalo General Military Hospital and Msata Military Training Base, March 2-12.
  • USAHC Ansbach and mission partners test readiness in MASCAL Exercise Army News 27 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Army Health Clinic (USAHC) Ansbach conducted a mass casualty (MASCAL) exercise at USAG Ansbach's Urlas Kaserne Wednesday, March 23, 2026.
  • Foreign Comparative Testing Remains Promising Potential for Future Projects Army News 27 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Army Combat Capabilities Development Command (DEVCOM) Armaments Center seeks not only to create new technologies for the warfighter, but also to leverage foreign technologies to get new capabilities into Soldiers' hands faster.
  • Sentinel Program advances with silo prototype US Air Force 27 Mar 2026 -- The LGM-35A Sentinel intercontinental ballistic missile program is rapidly moving from digital design to tangible reality, marked by the recent groundbreaking for a full-scale launch silo prototype in Promontory, Utah. This milestone marks a key step in modernizing the land-based leg of the nation's nuclear triad.
  • U.S., Guyana launch medical mission LAMAT 2026 SOUTHCOM 27 Mar 2026 -- U.S. and Guyanese officials gathered March 16, 2026, in Georgetown to officially launch the Guyana portion of the Lesser Antilles Medical Assistance Team (LAMAT) 2026 mission, marking another milestone in the nations' long-standing health partnership.
  • Closing the Sensor-to-Shooter Gap, One Dynamis Serial at a Time USMC News 27 Mar 2026 -- In an increasingly complex global landscape, the speed of decision-making on the battlefield is not just an advantage, it is the ultimate arbiter of victory. From March 3-20, Project Dynamis, the Marine Corps' engine for AI-enabled battle management, conducted its fifth iterative exercise concurrently across five sites coast-to-coast including Fort Carson, Colorado, and Charleston, South Carolina. "Dynamis Serial 005" previewed a future where a unified, multi-domain joint data mesh enables AI-enabled battle management command and control workflows.
  • CNO completes multi-stop Indo-Pacific engagement, reinforcing partnerships and forward presence US Navy Press Release 27 Mar 2026 -- Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle traveled more than 18,000 miles across the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command area of responsibility during a multi-stop visit to Australia and Singapore, underscoring the U.S. Navy's enduring commitment to allies, partners and forward-deployed forces in the region.

  • NATO Military Committee highlights Air and Space power during Allied Air Command visit NATO Allied Air Command 27 Mar 2026 -- Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, Chairman of the NATO Military Committee, and the NATO Military Committee visited Allied Air Command on March 25 and 26, 2026. The visit focused on the strategic importance of Allied Air and Space Power in maintaining collective deterrence and defence.

  • 10th anniversary of the National Guard President of Russia 27 Mar 2026 -- In the Grand Hall of the State Kremlin Palace, the President addressed a gala event marking the 10th anniversary of the National Guard of Russia.
  • Address on National Guard Day President of Russia 27 Mar 2026

  • Ukraine demonstrates advanced air defence capabilities to Lithuania and plans joint support for defence startups Ukraine MOD 27 Mar 2026 -- During a visit to Ukraine, a delegation from the Lithuanian Ministry of National Defence, led by Minister Robertas Kaunas, was briefed on the operation of the air defence system. Ukraine and Lithuania also discussed strengthening cooperation and enhancing support for defence startups.
  • Fast and manoeuvrable: the advantages of CVR(T) armoured vehicles to be delivered to Ukraine Ukraine MOD 27 Mar 2026 -- Ukraine will receive a new batch of CVR(T) armoured fighting vehicles from Latvia to equip a battalion-level unit. Vehicle deliveries have already commenced.
  • Updated e-Points system: military to be rewarded for sniper operations and lower-tier air defense missions Ukraine MOD 27 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of Defence continues to enhance the Army of Drones Bonus programme by expanding e-Points accrual and adding new operational areas: sniper operations, mobile fire groups, and army aviation. More e-Points will be awarded for innovative use of technology against the enemy.
  • The military can exchange e-Points for drone and technology components Ukraine MOD 27 Mar 2026 -- On the Brave1 Market, military units can now exchange e-Points not only for ready-made solutions—such as drones, electronic warfare (EW) equipment, and ground robotic systems—but also for components.
  • UK commits £100 million air defence package for Ukraine to protect cities and critical infrastructure UK MOD 27 Mar 2026 -- The UK will urgently commit an additional £100 million for air defence support to Ukraine, helping to defend the country from Russia's relentless attacks.
  • Our forces will intensify expertise exchange on air defence, says Minister of National Defence R. Kaunas in Kyiv Lithuania MND 27 Mar 2026 -- Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas is on a visit in Ukraine where he met with Minister of Defence of Ukraine Mykhailo Fedorov. R.Kaunas was thoroughly familiarized with functioning of Ukraine's air defence system and potential application of its elements in Lithuania.

  • Official Chinese media unveils world's first case of soft-body bionic manta ray submersible detecting simulated underwater explosive devices Global Times 27 Mar 2026 -- An official Chinese media report on Friday unveiled the world's first case of a soft-body bionic manta ray submersible detecting and locking onto simulated underwater explosive devices in dark waters with visibility of less than one meter.
  • Chinese peacekeepers survey lifeline in South Sudan PRC MND 27 Mar 2026 -- The engineering contingent assigned to the 16th Chinese Peacekeeping Horizontal Engineering Company to the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS) successfully completed a four-day survey mission of the Tonj-Romich[PH1.1] supply route.

  • Taiwan seeking U.S. consent on payment deferral for HIMARS CNA 27 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of National Defense has requested that the United States push back the payment deadline for additional high mobility artillery rocket systems (HIMARS) approved for sale to Taiwan, as funding bills for the systems remain in limbo, a defense official said Friday.

  • First Australian-built Boxer Combat Reconnaissance Vehicles roll off assembly line Australia DOD 27 Mar 2026 -- The Albanese Government is delivering on its commitment to deliver capabilities to the Australian Defence Force (ADF) at pace with the first batch of Australian‑built Boxer Combat Reconnaissance Vehicles (CRV) already rolling off the assembly line.

  • DAC clears proposals worth Rs 2.38 lakh crore to augment defence capabilities India PIB 27 Mar 2026 -- The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh on March 27, 2026, granted Acceptance of Necessity (AoN) for various proposals worth an estimated cost of about Rs 2.38 lakh crore. For the Indian Army, approval was accorded for the Air Defence Tracked System, Armoured Piercing Tank Ammunition, High Capacity Radio Relay, Dhanush Gun System and Runway Independent Aerial Surveillance System.
  • INDIAN NAVAL SHIP TRIKAND MAKES PORT CALL AT MAPUTO, MOZAMBIQUE India PIB 27 Mar 2026 -- INS Trikand, a frontline guided missile frigate of the Indian Navy, arrived at Maputo, Mozambique, on 26 Mar 2026 as part of her operational deployment to the South West Indian Ocean Region. The visit aims to further strengthen maritime cooperation and bilateral relations between India and Mozambique.
  • PERSONNEL FROM 16 FRIENDLY FOREIGN COUNTRIES COMPLETE HARBOUR TRAINING AS INDIAN NAVY STRENGTHENS REGIONAL MARITIME COOPERATION THROUGH 'IOS SAGAR' INITIATIVE India PIB 27 Mar 2026 -- Indian Navy's IOS SAGAR initiative achieved a significant milestone in advancing regional maritime cooperation with the successful completion of an intensive Harbour Training Phase at the Southern Naval Command, Kochi. The initiative reinforces India's commitment to collaborative security and capacity building in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
  • MoD inks Rs 858 crore contracts for Tunguska Air Defence Missile System & Inspection (Depot Level) of P8I Aircraft India PIB 27 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of Defence has signed contracts, worth a total of Rs 858 crore, for the procurement of Tunguska Air Defence Missile System and Inspection (Depot Level) of P8I Long-Range Maritime Reconnaissance Aircraft. The contracts were inked at Kartavya Bhawan-2, New Delhi on March 27, 2026.

  • Israeli opposition leader highlights chief of staff's warning of collapsing army Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- Israeli opposition leader Yair Lapid says that over the past 13 years of his presence in the regime's security institutions, he remembers no warnings more severe than the recent one by Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir about the occupation's collapsing military.

  • OCCAR Delivers Second MMCM System to the United Kingdom OCCAR 27 Mar 2026 -- OCCAR is pleased to announce the successful delivery of the second MMCM (Maritime Mine Counter Measures) Primary System, ADVENTURE, to the United Kingdom and the Royal Navy. This milestone follows the initial system deliveries in 2024 to France and 2025 to the UK, marking another significant step forward in the OCCAR managed MMCM Programme.

  • Minister Pevkur in Warsaw: Poland a Priority Partner for Estonia's Defence Industry Estonia MoD 27 Mar 2026 -- Estonian Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur met today in Warsaw with his Polish counterpart, Władysław Kosiniak-Kamysz, to discuss strengthening defence cooperation, the European security environment, and the development of the defence industry.

  • Six negative decisions on real estate transactions Finnish MoD 27 Mar 2026 -- On 27 March 2026, Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen denied six prospective buyers from outside the EU and EEA a permit to buy real estate in Finland.

  • Government to provide additional NOK 115 billion to strengthen Long-term Defence Plan Government of Norway 27 Mar 2026 -- The Norwegian Government will allocate an additional NOK 115 billion to the financial framework for the Long-term Defence Plan for the period up to 2036. NOK 31 billion of this will be provided by 2030. The Government is also proposing adjustments to the plan and is redefining some of the priorities.

  • Romania and Albania strengthen their military cooperation Romania - Ministry of National Defence 27 Mar 2026 -- On March 26 and 27, The Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, had an official meeting with the Chief of General Staff of the Republic of Albania's Armed Forces, Lieutenant General Arben Kingji, in Bucharest and Sibiu.

  • Joint statement by the Ministers of Defence of the Baltic countries on drone incidents Latvia MOD 27 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defence of Latvia: russia carries out information operation against Baltics Latvia MOD 27 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of Defence reports that russia is currently carrying out a large-scale, coordinated information operation against Latvia, Lithuania, and Estonia, claiming that the Baltic States are allowing their territories to be used for Ukrainian attacks against russia.

  • A look at the Defence news 16 - 22 March Netherlands Ministry of Defence 27 Mar 2026

  • Ministers of Defence of Poland and Estonia on security of the region and building drone capabilities Poland - Ministry of National Defence 27 Mar 2026 -- The Deputy Prime Minister has met with Hanno Pevkur, the Minister of Defence of Estonia, in Warsaw on 27 March.

  • The Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia met with French Prime Minister Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 27 Mar 2026 -- The Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia Suren Papikyan met with French Prime Minister Sébastien Le Corneille in Paris.
  • Suren Papikyan met with French Minister of Armed Forces and Veterans Affairs Catherine Vautrin in Paris Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 27 Mar 2026 -- The Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia Suren Papikyan met with French Minister of Armed Forces and Veterans Affairs Catherine Vautrin in Paris.

  • PH, France sign visiting forces pact Philippine News Agency 27 Mar 2026 -- Defense chiefs of the Philippines and France formally signed the Status of Visiting Forces Agreement (SOVFA) between the two nations, the Department of National Defense (DND) announced on Friday.

  • Cuba Will Defend Sovereignty at All Costs Despite US Energy Blockade — Deputy PM Sputnik 27 Mar 2026 -- Cuba will continue to defend its sovereignty at all costs, despite the massive negative effect of the US energy blockade, Cuban Deputy Prime Minister Oscar Perez-Oliva Fraga said on Friday.
  • Trump threatens "Cuba is next" following U.S. attacks on Venezuela, Iran People's Daily 28 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday threatened "Cuba is next" following U.S. attacks on Venezuela and Iran.

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio Remarks to Press US Dept. of State 27 Mar 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with The Republic of Marshall Islands Foreign Minister Kalani Kaneko US Dept. of State 27 Mar 2026

  • Operation Roaring Lion: Hundreds of Weapons Discovered Inside School in Southern Lebanon During Shayetet 13 Operation Israel Defense Forces 27 Mar 2026

  • 'Miracle': Lebanon's Hezbollah conducts over 100 anti-Israel operations in one day Press TV 27 Mar 2026 -- The Hezbollah resistance movement has carried out 103 retaliatory operations against Israeli targets inside Lebanon and the occupied Palestinian territories within the past 24 hours.

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory UNOCHA 27 Mar 2026
  • UNHCR calls for urgent support in Lebanon as humanitarian catastrophe looms UNHCR 27 Mar 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by Karolina Lindholm Billing, UNHCR Representative in Lebanon - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.
  • An average of 19,000 children displaced daily as escalating violence uproots 20 per cent of Lebanon's population in three weeks UNICEF 27 Mar 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by UNICEF Representative in Lebanon, Marcoluigi Corsi - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva
  • Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears UN News 27 Mar 2026 -- Almost one month since Israeli and US airstrikes on Iran began, sparking a wider regional war, UN agencies and partners on Friday highlighted the terror among civilians fleeing bombardment, with "no safe space" to go.

  • Commission scales up its humanitarian response in Lebanon to address urgent needs European External Action Service (EEAS) 27 Mar 2026 -- This week, the European Commission scaled up its humanitarian response in Lebanon, delivering nearly 150 tonnes of life-saving supplies to support people displaced by the deteriorating situation in the country.
  • EU support brings urgent aid to families forced to flee their homes in Lebanon European External Action Service (EEAS) 27 Mar 2026 -- UNHCR, the UN Refugee Agency, together with the European Union and the Governments of Ireland and Italy, welcomed today a humanitarian airbridge flight carrying urgently needed emergency relief items to support the growing number of forcibly displaced families across Lebanon.
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Explanation of Vote on the draft resolution on the Occupied Palestinian Territory UK FCDO 27 Mar 2026 -- UK Explanation of Vote on the draft resolution on the human rights situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory. Delivered by UK Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

    • Other Conflicts

  • PH, China resume bilateral talks on SCS Philippine News Agency 27 Mar 2026 -- The Philippines and China are set to resume and convene their bilateral talks on the South China Sea in Quangzhou, Fujian in China, on March 28, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) confirmed Friday.
  • PH Navy vessel avoids collision with Chinese warship in WPS Philippine News Agency 27 Mar 2026 -- The Philippine Navy ship BRP Benguet (LS-507) avoided a potential collision after a dangerous maneuver by a Chinese missile frigate while it was conducting a routine operation near Pag-asa Island in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) on March 25.

  • Syria: Statement by the Spokesperson on the report of the Independent International Commission of Inquiry on the July 2025 violence in Sweida European External Action Service (EEAS) 27 Mar 2026 -- The report of the United Nations Commission of Inquiry, documenting serious violations of international human rights law and international humanitarian law in Sweida in July 2025, is an important contribution to advance justice and the search for accountability in Syria.
  • UN Syria Commission releases report on July massacres and other grave violations committed in Suwayda and calls for expanded accountability measures OHCHR 27 Mar 2026 -- The Independent International Commission of Inquiry on the Syrian Arab Republic today released a new report on the brutal violence that tore through Suwayda in July, leaving more than 1,700 people dead and forcing nearly 200,000 from their homes.
  • Syria: UN Commission documents grave violations in July 2025 escalation in Sweida UN News 27 Mar 2026 -- More than 1,700 people were killed and nearly 200,000 more displaced in the July 2025 massacres in Sweida, Syria, UN human rights investigators said on Friday in a report that called for greater action towards accountability.
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Introductory Statement on the draft resolution on Syria UK FCDO 27 Mar 2026 -- UK Introductory Statement on the draft resolution for supporting human rights in Syria. Delivered by UK Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

  • Phone conversation between the Foreign Ministers of Armenia and Azerbaijan Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia 27 Mar 2026

  • EU Statement in response to the report by the Head of the OSCE Mission to Bosnia and Herzegovina European External Action Service (EEAS) 27 Mar 2026
  • Senators Shaheen, Grassley, Wicker, Durbin, Tillis, Warren, Representatives Turner and Wagner Press Administration to Impose Sanctions on Milorad Dodik US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 27 Mar 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chuck Grassley (R-IA), Roger Wicker (R-MS), Dick Durbin (D-IL), Thom Tillis (R-NC) and Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Representatives Mike Turner (R-OH) and Ann Wagner (R-MO) sent a letter to Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent calling on the Administration to impose sanctions on former Republika Srpska President Milorad Dodik and his associates, given their ongoing actions to threaten the territorial integrity of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The lawmakers warned that Mr. Dodik's continued secessionist rhetoric, coordination with foreign actors and efforts to undermine the Dayton Peace Agreement risk destabilizing the Western Balkans at a critical moment and urged the Administration to comply with new sanctions passed in the recent Fiscal Year 2026 National Defense Authorization Act. They also noted that the Administration's prior decision to remove sanctions on Mr. Dodik has emboldened his behavior and undermined U.S. credibility in the region.

  • The Republic of Costa Rica considered on Friday the autonomy initiative presented by Morocco "the most appropriate, serious, credible, and realistic basis for reaching a political solution," asserting that "autonomy under Moroccan sovereignty could be the most feasible solution for resolving" the regional dispute over the Moroccan Sahara. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 27 Mar 2026

  • Colombia: UN experts hail anti-mercenary law, warn of rising recruitment into armed conflicts OHCHR 27 Mar 2026 -- The Working Group on mercenaries hailed the signing of a new law in Colombia by President Petro which ratifies the 1989 International Convention on Mercenaries. It also warned of the risks of rising recruitment of Colombians abroad to armed conflict and private security roles.

  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the resolution on South Sudan UK FCDO 27 Mar 2026 -- UK Statement for the resolution on advancing human rights in South Sudan. Delivered by UK Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

News Reports

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna's comment on Russian disinformation Estonia MFA 27 Mar 2026 -- On 24-25 March 2026, Russia carried out a large-scale aerial attack against Ukraine, during which approximately 1,000 Russian missiles and drones were launched within 24 hours, making it one of the most extensive attacks since the start of the full-scale war. Such attacks demonstrate that Russia continues to pursue its unchanged objectives - subjugating the whole of Ukraine and upending European security - and that its talk of peace is not sincere.

  • USA: UN experts welcome Minnesota truth-seeking entity and urge nation-wide investigations OHCHR 27 Mar 2026 -- UN human rights experts* today welcomed the establishment of a process to document and report serious human rights violations allegedly committed in Minnesota by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and Customs and Border Protection (CBP) agents during Operation Metro Surge and Operation PARRIS. They called for action from authorities in other relevant jurisdictions.

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 27 Mar 2026 -- The President held a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council via videoconference.
  • Telephone conversation with President of Kyrgyzstan Sadyr Japarov President of Russia 27 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of Kyrgyzstan Sadyr Japarov.
  • Russia's Relations With European Countries in Crisis, Not at Moscow's Fault - Putin Sputnik 27 Mar 2026 -- Russia's relations with European countries are in crisis, and through no fault of Moscow, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Friday.

  • Xi chairs CPC leadership meeting to review regulations on work of local Party committees PRC MND 27 Mar 2026 -- The Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee on Friday held a meeting to deliberate on regulations on the work of local Party committees.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on March 27, 2026 PRC MofA 27 Mar 2026
  • China, US maintain communication over Trump's China visit, Chinese FM responds to question on more info about Trump's visit Global Times 27 Mar 2026 -- "China and the US have maintained communication on US President Donald Trump's visit to China," Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said in response to a foreign media reporter's question about whether there was more information on Trump's visit to China and whether the trip would take place on May 14-15.

  • Indictment alleges China party official interested in Taiwan legislative appointment CNA 27 Mar 2026 -- The indictment of China-born spouse Xu Chunying (徐春鶯) revealed that an official from the Revolutionary Committee of the Chinese Kuomintang in 2024 had inquired about the legislative prospects of Li Chen-hsiu (李貞秀) after Xu declined an at-large nomination by the Taiwan People's Party (TPP).
  • Chinese mainland slams Lai Ching-te authorities for manipulating judiciary People's Daily 27 Mar 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson on Thursday denounced Taiwan authorities led by Lai Ching-te for manipulating the judiciary to suppress political opponents.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 27 Mar 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Congratulations from Chinese President KCNA 27 Mar 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , president of the State Affairs Commission of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, received a congratulatory message from Xi Jinping, president of the People's Republic of China, on March 26.
  • Talks Held between President of State Affairs of DPRK and Belarusian President Treaty on Friendship and Cooperation between DPRK and Belarus Signed KCNA 27 Mar 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, had talks with Aleksandr Lukashenko, president of the Republic of Belarus, on an official visit to the DPRK, on March 26.
  • Belarusian President Leaves Pyongyang KCNA 27 Mar 2026 -- Comrade Aleksandr Lukashenko, president of the Republic of Belarus, left here by his private plane on March 26 after paying an official visit to the DPRK at the invitation of Comrade Kim Jong Un , president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea.
  • Reception Given in Welcome of Belarusian President KCNA 27 Mar 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, hosted a reception on March 26 in welcome of the visit of Aleksandr Lukashenko, president of the Republic of Belarus, to the DPRK.

  • Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake UN News 27 Mar 2026 -- A "new wave of global instability is hitting Myanmar at the worst possible moment," a UN official in the country warned on Friday, as increases in fuel, food, and fertilizer prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict push vulnerable families closer to hunger one year after a devastating earthquake.

  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (March 27, 2026) India MEA 27 Mar 2026

  • Foreign Ministers' Meeting of G7 Member States at the Abbaye des Vaux-de-Cernay (26 and 27 March 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 27 Mar 2026 -- Under the French Presidency of the G7, the first formal G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting was held on 26 and 27 March 2026, at the Abbaye des Vaux-de-Cernay, in the Yvelines Department (78).

  • G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Paris (Overview of the "Session 3: Cross-cutting Threats and Sovereignty") Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 27 Mar 2026
  • G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Paris (Overview of the "Session 4: Support for Ukraine") Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 27 Mar 2026
  • G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Paris (Overview of the "Session 5: Situation in Iran and the Surrounding Region") Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 27 Mar 2026
  • G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Paris (Overview of the"Session 6: Peace and Security") Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 27 Mar 2026
  • Japanese gov't approves stopgap funding amid struggles to pass fiscal 2026 budget People's Daily 27 Mar 2026 -- The Japanese government on Friday approved a stopgap budget of 8.56 trillion yen (about 54 billion U.S. dollars) to cover expenditures for the first 11 days of April, as it struggles to secure the enactment of the regular budget plan before the start of fiscal 2026 on Wednesday, local media reported.

  • Minister Anand meets with Germany's Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs at G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting Canada 27 Mar 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, today met with Johann Wadephul, Germany's Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs, at the G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay, France.
  • Minister Anand meets with Indian counterpart at G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting Canada 27 Mar 2026 -- The Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, yesterday met with Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, Minister of External Affairs of India, while attending the G7 Foreign Ministers' Meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay, France.

  • Press conference - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 27 Mar 2026

  • EU Statement in response to the Report by the Director of ODIHR, Maria Telalian European External Action Service (EEAS) 27 Mar 2026

  • The President met with Estonian President Alar Karis in Tartu President of the Republic of Lithuania 27 Mar 2026 -- On Friday, President Gitanas Nausėda met with President Alar Karis of Estonia in Tartu.
  • The President: Current changes require critical thinking, intellectual flexibility, and the ability to act in unpredictable conditions President of the Republic of Lithuania 27 Mar 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda visited the Baltic Defence College in Tartu, Estonia, on Friday.
